title: Here are the dates for the Patriots’ offseason workout schedule
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots will begin phase one of their offseason program on April 20 with conditioning drills and meetings.
Patriots' OTAs are scheduled for May 27, May 29, June 1-2, June 4, June 8-9, and June 11.
A mandatory minicamp is set for June 15-17, concluding the offseason program before July's training camp.

title: Patriots double up on edge rushers in latest ESPN mock draft
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The ESPN mock draft projects the Patriots to select edge rushers in the first two rounds.
Cashius Howell from Texas A&M is predicted to join the Patriots with the 31st pick, boosting pass rush.
Gabe Jacas, an edge rusher from Illinois, is projected to be selected in the second round by the Patriots.

title: For a struggling Red Sox team, Friday’s opener at Fenway Park provided a fresh start
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: The Boston Red Sox defeated the San Diego Padres 5-2 in their home opener at Fenway Park.
Pitcher Sonny Gray threw six strong innings, while Willson Contreras hit a winning home run.
Caleb Durbin broke his hitless streak with an RBI single, signaling a fresh start for the team.

title: North Andover police working to get body cameras after Kelsey Fitzsimmons shooting
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: North Andover is pursuing grant funding to implement a body-worn camera program for police officers, following a notable shooting incident.
Kelsey Fitzsimmons was acquitted after being charged with assault in an armed confrontation at her home with a fellow officer.
Judge noted lack of body cameras affected trial outcome; state grants have supported body camera programs in 144 departments since 2021.

title: Xander Bogaerts talks Roman Anthony, pressure from fans in his return to Fenway Park
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Xander Bogaerts returned to Fenway Park with the Padres, receiving a standing ovation from Red Sox fans.
Bogaerts reflected on the high expectations and pressures of playing in Boston compared to San Diego.
He praised Red Sox outfielder Roman Anthony, noting the increased responsibility after signing an eight-year contract.

title: Step into Boston’s past this season
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Boston's Freedom Trail, a 2.5-mile path connecting 16 historical sites, attracts over 4 million visitors annually.
Six Flags New England introduces Quantum Accelerator, a dual-launch straddle coaster, opening April 11 for season pass holders.
Restaurant Week Portsmouth & the Seacoast features special three-course menus, priced at $32, $42, and $52, from April 9-18.

title: Singer Vanessa Carlton’s noise complaint against R.I. neighbor ends up before state Supreme Court
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Vanessa Carlton and husband John McCauley are involved in a noise complaint case in Rhode Island Supreme Court.
Carlton obtained a 2024 injunction against neighbors for noise and alleged harassment, which is now being appealed.
Neighbors Artak Avagyan and Lee Beausoleil dispute the injunction, claiming they've been considerate and the order lacks specificity.

title: Delta is adding winter service between Boston and Rome in 2027
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Delta Air Lines will introduce winter flights between Boston and Rome starting January 2027.
Flights will operate four times weekly from January until daily service resumes in March.
Flight fares for the 2027 service go on sale starting April 4, 2026.

title: He’s running his second Boston Marathon for Brigham and Women’s Hospital
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Joe Greeley, 30, from Charlestown, is running his second Boston Marathon for Brigham and Women's Hospital's Stepping Strong.
Greeley first ran the Boston Marathon in 2024, finding it both challenging and rewarding, particularly supporting a charity.
Inspired by the 2025 marathon, Greeley decided to run again, encouraged by conversations with Stepping Strong team organizers.

title: ‘Real Housewives of Rhode Island’: Everything that happened in Episode 1
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: 'Real Housewives of Rhode Island' premiered with six of seven cast members, establishing a drama-filled environment.
Alicia Carmody and fiancé Billy Kitsilis face family pressure to marry after nine years engaged.
Controversy arises at Rosie Woods DiMare's picnic over polygamy comments, leading to a heated argument.
